name: Berrocal - Río Tinto Loop Trail
name_original: Las Zorrozuelas – trail run circular desde Berrocal
description: This difficult 9.5-mile (15.4 km) trail run near Berrocal offers challenging terrain, river views, and panoramic vistas from Río Tinto Viewp
region_intro: Jogging routes around Berrocal are characterized by a diverse landscape featuring a mountain atmosphere, shady forests, and extensive holm oak pastures. The region's terrain includes ancient mining areas and the unique Río Tinto, providing varied surfaces for running. These natural attributes make Berrocal an appealing destination for outdoor activities, offering both open spaces and sheltered paths. The area's rich scenic and cultural heritage adds an element of historical exploration to running pursuits. Best jogging routes around Berrocal The most popular jogging route is Peña del Hierro y nacimiento del Río Tinto - running circular desde Nerva, a 9.6 miles (15.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 52 minutes to complete. This challenging circular route explores the historic mining landscapes and the source of the Río Tinto. Another top favourite among local runners is Río Tinto Loop from Berrocal, a difficult 9.5 miles (15.3 km) path. This route offers varied terrain as it loops through the distinctive environment surrounding the Río Tinto. Local runners also love the Berrocal - Río Tinto Loop Trail, a 9.6 miles (15.4 km) trail leading through the unique landscapes of the Río Tinto area, often completed in about 2 hours 2 minutes. Jogging around Berrocal is defined by shady forests, holm oak pastures, and the distinctive Río Tinto, offering a mix of natural environments. The network provides options for runners seeking challenging routes through varied terrain. The routes in Berrocal are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 130 runners have used komoot to explore Berrocal's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in the Berrocal area?
A: There are &lt;em&gt;over 8 dedicated running routes&lt;/em&gt; around Berrocal, offering a variety of experiences for runners. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Berrocal?
A: The running trails in Berrocal feature a diverse landscape, including a mountain atmosphere, shady forests, and extensive holm oak pastures. You'll also encounter unique ancient mining areas and the distinctive Río Tinto, providing varied surfaces from natural paths to more rugged terrain.

Q: Are there any easy running routes for beginners in Berrocal?
A: While many routes in Berrocal are rated as moderate to difficult, there are options that can be adapted for beginners. The region's varied terrain means you can find flatter sections within the holm oak pastures, though dedicated easy routes are less common. We recommend checking the route details on komoot for elevation profiles before you go.

Q: Are there circular running trails in Berrocal?
A: Yes, Berrocal offers several excellent circular running trails. A popular choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984536943/pena-del-hierro-y-nacimiento-del-rio-tinto-running-circular-desde-nerva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Hierro y nacimiento del Río Tinto - running circular desde Nerva&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 9.6-mile loop exploring historic mining landscapes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984552197/berrocal-rio-tinto-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Berrocal - Río Tinto Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, also a 9.6-mile circular route through the unique Río Tinto area.

Q: What interesting landmarks or sights can I see along the running routes?
A: Many running routes in Berrocal pass by significant historical and natural landmarks. You can explore the unique landscapes of the Río Tinto, and some trails are near sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5287308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corta Atalaya Mine&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5082202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Touristic Railway for Rio Tinto Mines&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5345695" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cachán Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, offering a blend of natural beauty and industrial heritage.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Berrocal?
A: Generally, the natural areas around Berrocal are welcoming to dogs, especially on trails that pass through forests and open pastures. However, it's always best to check local regulations for specific routes or protected areas, and ensure your dog is on a leash and you clean up after them. The varied terrain and open spaces are often ideal for running with canine companions.

Q: Are there any running routes suitable for families with children?
A: While many routes are rated difficult, families can find sections of trails through the holm oak pastures or along the less strenuous parts of the Río Tinto area that are suitable for a family run or walk. For a full family outing, consider shorter, flatter segments or look for routes specifically marked as easy on komoot. Always check the distance and elevation before heading out with children.

Q: When is the best time of year to go running in Berrocal?
A: The presence of shady forests and holm oak pastures makes Berrocal appealing for running during various seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, while the shady areas provide respite from the sun during warmer months. Winter running is also possible, with the region's mild climate, though checking local weather conditions is always advisable.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Berrocal?
A: The komoot community highly rates the running experience in Berrocal, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the unique blend of natural beauty, the historical mining landscapes, and the varied terrain that keeps runs interesting. The peaceful escape from more bustling areas and the opportunity to explore a rich cultural heritage are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there longer running options for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. Experienced runners looking for a challenge will find several longer and more difficult routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24522911" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cachán Bridge – Riscos Altos Viewpoint loop from Los Frailes&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 20.9-mile (33.7 km) route with significant elevation gain, offering a substantial workout and expansive views.

Q: What kind of cultural heritage can I explore while running in Berrocal?
A: Berrocal boasts a rich scenic and cultural heritage. The region is known for its ancient mining areas, particularly around the Río Tinto, which has a history dating back to the Tartessian civilization. Running through these areas allows you to witness traces of this past, including old mining structures and unique landscapes shaped by centuries of human activity, adding a historical dimension to your run.

Q: Is there parking available near the running trailheads?
A: While specific parking information varies by trailhead, many routes in natural areas like Berrocal typically have designated parking spots or roadside areas where you can leave your vehicle. For detailed parking information for a specific route, it's best to check the route description on komoot, as users often provide tips on access and parking.
tips: Beautiful views of the Rio Tinto valley from this panoramic point. The river provides that ocher color and together with the environment that surrounds it, they make up an almost Martian landscape, from another planet.
From the bridge over the Gallego stream there is a slight ascent, smooth except for a curved section and with a slope of almost 8%. The landscape has recovered well after the 2004 fire.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this trail run?
faqs.answer: This trail run is designed to be completed in approximately 2 hours and 2 minutes. However, individual times can vary based on fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult. It covers a distance of over 15 km with significant elevation changes, making it suitable for experienced trail runners with good physical conditioning.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: Based on similar trails in the region, you can expect varied and challenging terrain. This often includes rocky and irregular surfaces, potentially with loose stones, and significant elevation changes. Some sections might be narrow or exposed, passing through forested areas or alongside rivers.

faqs.question: What natural features or viewpoints can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse natural beauty. You'll pass by the Río Tinto Viewpoint, providing scenic vistas. You'll also encounter the Arroyo Gallego, adding to the natural charm of the route.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Vía Verde del Río Tinto, Camino de las Eras, Aldea Las Delgadas a Via Verde del Río Tinto, Puente Cachá, and Carril Aldea Las Delgadas a Via Verde del Río Tinto.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the challenging terrain and potential for varied conditions, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant times for trail running in this region. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring cooler temperatures and potentially slicker surfaces.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for this exact trailhead are not available, routes starting from Berrocal typically have parking options within or on the outskirts of the village. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this trail is located within the Río Tinto Protected Landscape, dogs are generally permitted but must be kept on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the Río Tinto Protected Landscape?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for general access to trails within the Río Tinto Protected Landscape. However, always respect local regulations and signage, especially concerning protected areas.

faqs.question: What regulations should I be aware of when trail running in the Río Tinto Protected Landscape?
faqs.answer: When trail running in a protected landscape like Río Tinto, it's important to stay on marked trails, carry out all your trash, and avoid disturbing wildlife or plant life. Camping and fires are usually restricted, and dogs must be leashed. Always check for any specific seasonal restrictions or advisories before your visit.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this trail run?
faqs.answer: For a difficult trail run of this length, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, appropriate trail running shoes with good grip, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing suitable for changing weather conditions. A first-aid kit and a fully charged phone are also recommended.
